title: Chicklet

jokes.text: American University sounds like a made-up college in Egypt.
jokes.setup: Discussion of donating to American University for a library
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comparison to fictional foreign institution
jokes.explanation: Strong character-specific dismissiveness with good economy. Classic Selina snobbery delivered efficiently.

jokes.text: You understand that, Amy, that even Richard has trouble understanding you?
jokes.setup: Amy speaking unclearly about library details
jokes.punchline: Using Richard (known for confusion) as benchmark for clarity
jokes.explanation: Uses established character trait (Richard's confusion) as comparison point for insult. Well-structured putdown.

jokes.text: Even that sanctimonious fraud Carter had a library.
jokes.setup: Listing presidents who had libraries
jokes.punchline: Harsh characterization of Carter while making her point
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate political disdain. Shows Selina's bitterness toward other presidents.

jokes.text: American University, it's like American Univer-shit.
jokes.setup: Continuing criticism of American University
jokes.punchline: Childish portmanteau insult
jokes.explanation: Lazy wordplay relying on profanity substitution. Feels forced rather than clever.

jokes.text: I also do wallpaper.
jokes.setup: Volunteer event with scripted pleasantries
jokes.punchline: Random, inappropriate personal detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of awkward non-sequitur. Brief but memorably weird.

jokes.text: I look like I'm trying out for 'The Price is Right.'
jokes.setup: Wearing volunteer event T-shirt
jokes.punchline: Specific game show comparison
jokes.explanation: Good visual reference that captures both the look and Selina's horror at appearing common.

jokes.text: God Almighty, I wish I'd been assassinated in office.
jokes.setup: Complaining about current circumstances
jokes.punchline: Extreme preference for martyrdom over current situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation that perfectly captures Selina's self-pity and vanity. Shocking but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Although, with my luck, I'd be then crippled for life, sitting in a wheelchair in my reading room with my name on the thing.
jokes.setup: Wishing for assassination
jokes.punchline: Even martyrdom would go wrong for her
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that shows even her fantasy of martyrdom is pathetic. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: They haven't agreed to putting your name on anything, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Selina fantasizing about her named reading room
jokes.punchline: Reality check that deflates the fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good puncturing of Selina's delusion. Amy's deadpan delivery of harsh reality.

jokes.text: Oh, God, I hate every food ever, from everywhere.
jokes.setup: Discussion about disgusting food
jokes.punchline: Absurdly comprehensive food hatred
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Selina's negative worldview and tendency to extremes.

jokes.text: Have you tried ice cream? Ugh.
jokes.setup: Gary suggesting ice cream as universally liked food
jokes.punchline: Even ice cream disgusts her
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline with Gary's innocent suggestion meeting Selina's impossible standards.

jokes.text: Is this the Hall of Half-Term Wonders?
jokes.setup: Seeing her wax figure next to Gerald Ford
jokes.punchline: Dismissive categorization of short-term presidents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant coinage that captures both historical knowledge and self-aware bitterness. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I thought that was Craig T. Nelson.
jokes.setup: Looking at Gerald Ford wax figure
jokes.punchline: Mistaking him for sitcom actor
jokes.explanation: Good visual joke about the blandness of both figures. Unexpected pop culture reference.

jokes.text: The real one was made of wood.
jokes.setup: Question about why Ford has a wax figure
jokes.punchline: Implying Ford himself was wooden/lifeless
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'wooden' personality. Perfect economy and political snark.

jokes.text: Just do it before I melt down that whole collection and use it to wax Madame Tussauds' big, fat, nasty snatch!
jokes.setup: Demanding action on wax figure placement
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely creative threat involving institution's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Creatively vulgar escalation, though relies somewhat on shock value. Very Selina-like imagery.

jokes.text: Trust me, Amy, it was not the heart attack that depressed your dad.
jokes.setup: Amy mentioning her father's post-heart attack depression
jokes.punchline: Implying Amy herself was the depressing factor
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina cruelty disguised as consolation. Good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: I just feel like I'm a thick, dark fog and everyone disappoints me and nothing works out, and what's the point of anything anyway?
jokes.setup: Claiming she's not depressed
jokes.punchline: Immediately listing classic depression symptoms
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic self-contradiction. Selina's complete lack of self-awareness on display.

jokes.text: 'Cause I'm getting 14 hours of sleep a night.
jokes.setup: Denying sleep problems while listing depression symptoms
jokes.punchline: Absurdly excessive sleep amount
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the depression denial. The specific number makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Both of them went to town on me. It was brutal. I cried so hard, I threw up, but it was the best thing I've ever done.
jokes.setup: Recommending couples therapy
jokes.punchline: Describing therapeutic breakdown in disturbing detail
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Mike's oversharing tendency. Escalates to physical symptoms.

jokes.text: You're in therapy? Since when? Since I was 13.
jokes.setup: Selina surprised Catherine is in therapy
jokes.punchline: Revealing decades-long therapy duration
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that explains Catherine's issues while implicating Selina as the cause.

jokes.text: Well, what you really needed was a dermatologist.
jokes.setup: Learning Catherine has been in therapy since 13
jokes.punchline: Deflecting to superficial concern about appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina response - avoiding emotional responsibility by focusing on superficial flaws.

jokes.text: Well, my father and I both hate eating butterscotch, but love to say it. Butterscotch.
jokes.setup: Asked about hereditary family issues
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific shared quirk about candy name
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Richard response. The demonstration makes it even better. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, no, it's a graveyard that sells gum.
jokes.setup: Someone saying bookstore isn't a library
jokes.punchline: Describing bookstore as dead medium with convenience store elements
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor for dying print industry. Efficient and memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: I show assholes the door.
jokes.setup: Asked what chores she does to deserve allowance
jokes.punchline: Describing dealing with her mother's visitors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Catherine line - bitter but accurate. Great economy and character insight.

jokes.text: Here's a statement... your investment is doing quite well.
jokes.setup: Catherine asking for investment statement
jokes.punchline: Verbal 'statement' instead of document
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Andrew's sleazy evasiveness. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: And then we just hired the least fuckable press secretary we could find.
jokes.setup: Firing Louise Kellog for affair with Andrew
jokes.punchline: Hiring based on sexual unattractiveness
jokes.explanation: Dark but logical response to betrayal. Shows Selina's calculating nature.

jokes.text: Huh, that's actually right when I started working with you.
jokes.setup: Selina describing hiring least fuckable press secretary
jokes.punchline: Mike realizing he was that secretary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal that recontextualizes Mike's entire relationship with Selina. Perfect timing and delivery.

jokes.text: which is a line of prison inmates standing on each other's shoulders trying to sodomize you.
jokes.setup: Explaining fundraising law violations lead to prison
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific prison assault imagery
jokes.explanation: Creatively disturbing escalation, though relies on shock value. Memorable imagery.

jokes.text: I'm the daughter. Gross.
jokes.setup: Jonah calling daughter 'smoking hot'
jokes.punchline: Correcting and expressing disgust
jokes.explanation: Standard correction comedy, efficient but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: More crime means more for-profit prisons. We're gonna lock 'em up and we're gonna fry 'em.
jokes.setup: Discussing daylight savings and crime
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic description of profiting from human misery
jokes.explanation: Shows Sherman's callous capitalism. Dark but fits the character's moral blindness.

jokes.text: If you kill them, they can't get arrested again. That's how we make our money.
jokes.setup: Father's enthusiasm for execution
jokes.punchline: Pointing out business flaw in death penalty
jokes.explanation: Smart economic logic applied to disturbing situation. Shows daughter is smarter than father.

jokes.text: Failure to thrive at birth... fuck that. Puberty at 19. When it hit, it hit like a fucking thunderbolt. Third grade reading level in the tenth grade.
jokes.setup: Defending himself against underestimation
jokes.punchline: List of actual failures presented as triumphs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah delusion - turning obvious deficits into proof of eventual success. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Mother up and sold her. She hated all animals that she couldn't eat or wear.
jokes.setup: Talking about beloved horse Chicklet
jokes.punchline: Mother's coldly practical view of animals
jokes.explanation: Good insight into family dysfunction. Shows where Selina gets her callousness.

jokes.text: In 10 years, we'll be taking vacations together.
jokes.setup: Selina saying she's not bad once you know her after 20 years
jokes.punchline: Projecting even more distant future bonding
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the time scale joke. Shows Mike's desperate optimism.

jokes.text: What is this, Yonkers?
jokes.setup: Mike asking for sausage patties
jokes.punchline: Dismissive geographic reference for common food
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina snobbery about food. Good economy but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Oh, that Chinese one?
jokes.setup: Asked to write recommendation for Mike's daughter Ellen
jokes.punchline: Casually racist identification
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's casual racism and inability to remember people properly. Cringe but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Grandma Splett always said that self-pleasure was a sin like microwaves or laughter.
jokes.setup: Explaining religious upbringing about masturbation
jokes.punchline: Grouping normal things as sins
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Richard backstory. The escalation from microwaves to laughter is brilliant.

jokes.text: No. Worse comes to worst, I'll burn in hell like Grandma Splett.
jokes.setup: Asked if he needs a minute before providing sample
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning grandmother's damnation
jokes.explanation: Great callback that implies Grandma broke her own rules. Perfect Richard logic.

jokes.text: Honestly, it was one less thing to worry about.
jokes.setup: Jonah asking if missing testicle freaked her out
jokes.punchline: Practical response to intimate concern
jokes.explanation: Perfectly deadpan response that's both honest and devastating. Great economy.

jokes.text: They're fucking her in the ass?
jokes.setup: Being told people are taking advantage of Selina's wax figure
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of euphemistic description
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation comedy, though relies on shock value.

jokes.text: You're as useless as a dick at a roller derby.
jokes.setup: Gary failing to fix wax figure situation
jokes.punchline: Creative uselessness comparison
jokes.explanation: Creative and specific insult. Good visual imagery and economy.

jokes.text: Zero anal access. How's that for a new deal?
jokes.setup: Describing FDR-Selina wax figure interaction
jokes.punchline: Pun on FDR's New Deal program
jokes.explanation: Clever historical pun applied to vulgar situation. Good wordplay.

jokes.text: for a president who is currently more famous for being a jizz sock for Times Square tourists
jokes.setup: Describing Selina's current reputation problems
jokes.punchline: Grotesque metaphor for wax figure abuse
jokes.explanation: Vivid and disgusting imagery that captures the degradation. Creative but relies on shock.

jokes.text: Your dad sold it to get the IRS off of his back.
jokes.setup: Selina's fond memory of mother selling her horse
jokes.punchline: Reality that father sold horse for tax problems
jokes.explanation: Good reality check that destroys Selina's childhood narrative.

jokes.text: Died right on top of her. What a way to go. She was built, my God.
jokes.setup: Describing how Barbara died with Selina's father
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate appreciation of dead woman's body
jokes.explanation: Appropriately creepy for Uncle George character. Dark but fits the tone.

jokes.text: you know, because your dad was always taking her to the doctor.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Barbara had no children
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to abortions
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation delivered casually. Shows the family's moral compromises.

jokes.text: Pay me! Pay me, bitch!
jokes.setup: Asked to get mad while destroying office
jokes.punchline: Mike's pathetic attempt at anger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - even his anger is about money. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Why do shoes cost so much money?! I wish I had peed first. Why do I always wait so long to pee? I'm a grown man! I shouldn't eat when I'm full!
jokes.setup: Mike getting increasingly worked up while destroying furniture
jokes.punchline: Random life complaints mixed with destruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that reveals Mike's everyday frustrations through destruction therapy. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: I shouldn't eat when I'm full!
jokes.setup: Continuing destruction while expressing anger
jokes.punchline: Bizarre self-criticism about eating habits
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random anger topic. Shows Mike's scattered thought process.

jokes.text: Don't you see? I had no choice but to go into politics and be extraordinary and a sex symbol.
jokes.setup: Discovering family dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Self-serving interpretation of trauma as destiny
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina delusion - turning family trauma into justification for greatness. Excellent character work.

jokes.text: Yes, Danny, I know that we're not actually fucking because you're not a billionaire and I don't wanna catch anything.
jokes.setup: Dan asking if Jane knows they're not actually having affair
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about her standards and his inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Devastating putdown that establishes Jane's character perfectly. Cold and calculating.

jokes.text: by two tits with a degree from American University over there.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she needs to stay relevant
jokes.punchline: Reducing replacement to body parts and education
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier American University joke while showing Jane's cynicism about her industry.

jokes.text: So you don't scrape your head, you giant barbecue fork.
jokes.setup: Jonah asking why they always raise debt ceiling
jokes.punchline: Explaining concept while insulting Jonah's height
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that references Jonah's height. Good visual metaphor.

jokes.text: No, I'm gonna look like that old popcorn homo.
jokes.setup: Told to switch to bow ties
jokes.punchline: Reference to Orville Redenbacher with slur
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's casual bigotry and poor judgment. Offensive but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Well, ironically, I have bigger tits.
jokes.setup: Discussion of female form being formidable adversary
jokes.punchline: Dry self-assessment of his own physique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent line - analytical and self-deprecating. Great economy and delivery.

jokes.text: What does Rumpleforeskin want?
jokes.setup: Told Sherman Tanz wants a favor
jokes.punchline: Crude nickname for wealthy donor
jokes.explanation: Creative portmanteau insult. Shows Selina's contempt for donors despite needing them.

jokes.text: It's just, like, a woman's face with sunglasses on.
jokes.setup: Selina asking what's on Catherine's sweater
jokes.punchline: Banal description of fashion design
jokes.explanation: Good Catherine response - earnest but missing the point of Selina's disapproval.

jokes.text: So, people were trying to have sex with my statue. Domestic terrorism.
jokes.setup: Learning about wax figure inappropriate touching
jokes.punchline: Classifying sexual assault of statue as terrorism
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Selina's tendency to dramatize everything involving herself.

jokes.text: Dominoes.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if he's all right
jokes.punchline: Random single word response
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random Gary response. Maximum economy with mysterious meaning.

tmdb_overview: Selina settles on a location for her presidential library. Dan lands in a gossip column. Mike and Selina work on her book. Jonah tries to cement a big donation. Gary manages a mini-crisis at Madame Tussauds.
insight: 81-point episode rides 59 jokes in 38 minutes, banking on character chaos over setup precision.
